entryPointCandidates.name: main.py
entryPointCandidates.summary: FastAPI application entry point wiring all HTTP routes for PharmAgent: sessions, dataset upload, chat, workflows, every PK/NCA/BE/NLME/SCM analysis, async jobs, exports (CSV/control/CDISC), skills, adversarial review, reports, and the SPA frontend mount, with bearer auth and a request-context/error envelope.

entryPointCandidates.name: __init__.py
entryPointCandidates.summary: Empty package marker making `app` an importable Python package.

entryPointCandidates.name: compartmental.py
entryPointCandidates.summary: Closed-form one- and two-compartment oral PK models (single-dose and steady-state) plus least-squares fitting of individual subjects and dose-grouped cohorts.

entryPointCandidates.name: pk_fit.py
entryPointCandidates.summary: Individual (naive-pooled / two-stage) fitting of structural PK and PK-PD models by nonlinear least squares, with Jacobian-based RSEs, dataset-level fitting, and AIC model comparison.

entryPointCandidates.name: regulatory_tools.py
entryPointCandidates.summary: Generates an ICH/FDA Module 2.7.2 (Summary of Clinical Pharmacology) Word document from analysis state, assembling narrative sections (background, NCA, popPK, special populations, DDI, conclusions) and standardized tables.

documentation.name: requirements.txt
documentation.summary: Pinned production dependency manifest for the FastAPI backend: web stack (fastapi, uvicorn, starlette, pydantic, pydantic-settings), numeric/scientific compute (pandas, numpy, scipy), DOCX export (python-docx), multipart uploads, and the anthropic LLM client.

documentation.name: requirements-dev.txt
documentation.summary: Development and test tooling manifest, excluded from the production image: includes runtime deps via -r requirements.txt, plus pytest, httpx (test client), ruff (linter), and pip-audit (dependency vulnerability scanner).

documentation.name: README.md
documentation.summary: Explains the reference-validation suite: how it drives the shipping compute path on canonical public datasets (Theophylline) to assert literature concordance, cross-method consistency, and optional exact tool concordance.

infrastructure.name: Dockerfile
infrastructure.summary: Multi-stage Docker build for the PharmAgent FastAPI backend: a builder stage installs pinned deps into a venv, and a slim runtime stage copies the venv plus app code, runs as a non-root user, and serves uvicorn on port 8000 with a /api/health healthcheck.

infrastructure.name: builder
infrastructure.summary: First build stage (python:3.13-slim) that creates a virtualenv at /opt/venv and installs pinned requirements, keeping build tooling out of the final image.

infrastructure.name: runtime
infrastructure.summary: Final runtime stage (python:3.13-slim) that copies the prebuilt venv and app/sample_data, sets data-dir env vars, runs as non-root user 'pharma', exposes port 8000, and launches uvicorn for app.main:app.

infrastructure.name: .dockerignore
infrastructure.summary: Docker build-context exclusion list that keeps virtualenvs, caches, tests, local data, SQLite DBs, secrets, and git metadata out of the image build context.

config.name: .env.example
config.summary: Template environment file documenting all PHARMAGENT_* runtime settings (optional Anthropic LLM key/model, SQLite storage paths, bearer-token auth, CORS origins, branding). All vars are optional — the app defaults to keyless MockLLM and open dev mode.

config.name: ruff.toml
config.summary: Ruff linter configuration for the backend: 120-char line length, py313 target, and a curated high-signal rule set (pyflakes, pycodestyle, import-order, pyupgrade) with stylistic rules (E701/E702/E731/E741) and bugbear deliberately disabled for the compact numeric code style.

config.name: .understandignore
config.summary: Ignore-pattern file scoping the Understand-Anything codebase scan, excluding data, caches, notebooks, and the retired graphify output.

config.name: scan-result.json
config.summary: Project-scanner inventory artifact for the Understand-Anything pipeline, listing every file with detected language, line count, and file category.

list.name: API & Entry
list.description: FastAPI application bootstrap and HTTP surface: app entrypoint (main.py), runtime settings (config.py), top-level workflow wiring (workflows.py), and DB package init that mount the agentic pharmacometrics service.

list.name: Agent Layer
list.description: LLM agent orchestration: the supervisor that drives multi-step pharmacometrics reasoning, the base agent abstraction, and the agent/tool definitions registry.

list.name: Tool Layer
list.description: Agent-callable tool implementations (PK model, NCA, BE, dose-proportionality, compartmental, poppk, report, regulatory, QC, review, data tools) plus the tool base/registry that bridge agents to the deterministic compute engine.

list.name: Compute Engine
list.description: Deterministic PK/PKPD numerical core: structural PK models, simulation/forecast, NCA (incl. steady-state), NLME (FOCE-I/SAEM/poppk), bioequivalence, dose-proportionality, dose-sweep, VPC, diagnostics, adversarial checks, and NONMEM export.

list.name: Core Orchestration & Infrastructure
list.description: Cross-cutting runtime services: the orchestrator, SHA-256 audit chain, background job runner, persistence store, LLM client, pharm-state, provenance/reproducibility, CDISC/exporters, schema extraction, skills, and logging config.

list.name: Test Suite
list.description: Pytest suite covering compute modules, API, orchestrator, audit/state, NLME, VPC, and the published-literature reference-validation tests (Theophylline) plus shared fixtures.

list.name: Infrastructure
list.description: Containerization and deployment: the multi-stage Dockerfile (builder and runtime stages) and the .dockerignore that package the backend for uvicorn-based serving.

list.name: Project Config & Docs
list.description: Project-level configuration (.env.example, ruff.toml, understand-anything configs/scan), dependency manifests (requirements*.txt), reference-test README, and the SQLite database artifact supporting the build and tooling environment.

file:app/__init__.py.name: __init__.py
file:app/__init__.py.summary: Empty package marker making `app` an importable Python package.

file:app/agents/__init__.py.name: __init__.py
file:app/agents/__init__.py.summary: Empty package marker making `app.agents` an importable Python package.

file:app/compute/__init__.py.name: __init__.py
file:app/compute/__init__.py.summary: Empty package marker making `app.compute` an importable Python package.

file:app/agents/base.py.name: base.py
file:app/agents/base.py.summary: Defines the Agent base class and AgentResult: an agent owns a system prompt and bound tools, mutating PharmState only through the audited, write-checked tool registry in a bounded tool-use loop.

class:app/agents/base.py:Agent.name: Agent
class:app/agents/base.py:Agent.summary: Base agent owning a system prompt and registry-bound tools; runs a bounded tool-use turn that executes tools (enforcing audit and write-access) and never calls other agents.

class:app/agents/base.py:AgentResult.name: AgentResult
class:app/agents/base.py:AgentResult.summary: Dataclass bundling an agent turn's resulting state, messages, and tool calls.

file:app/agents/definitions.py.name: definitions.py
file:app/agents/definitions.py.summary: Phase-1 roster of domain agent definitions (system prompts and descriptions) used by the supervisor for routing and by the orchestrator to instantiate agents.

file:app/agents/supervisor.py.name: supervisor.py
file:app/agents/supervisor.py.summary: Level-0 supervisor that routes a user message to a domain agent via weighted keyword scoring, falling back to LLM classification when ambiguous.

class:app/agents/supervisor.py:Superviso.name: Supervisor
class:app/agents/supervisor.py:Superviso.summary: Two-stage router: weighted keyword scoring over domain terms, then LLM classification on ambiguity; bypassed when a workflow template drives routing.

file:app/compute/adversarial.py.name: adversarial.py
file:app/compute/adversarial.py.summary: Adversarial review engine: a clean-context skeptic that independently and deterministically refutes analysis results, recomputing key NCA/NLME quantities from raw data with method-agnostic arithmetic to catch bugs in the primary code.

function:app/compute/adversarial.py:_nca.name: _nca_internal
function:app/compute/adversarial.py:_nca.summary: Internal NCA consistency checks (e.g. AUCinf below AUClast, non-positive CL/F, extrapolation out of range) emitting severity-graded findings.

function:app/compute/adversarial.py:_nca.name: _nca_dose_monotonic
function:app/compute/adversarial.py:_nca.summary: Flags non-monotonic dose-vs-exposure trends across dose groups as a medium-severity finding.

function:app/compute/adversarial.py:_nlm.name: _nlme_refute
function:app/compute/adversarial.py:_nlm.summary: Refutes NLME fits by flagging over-parameterization and non-converged results.

function:app/compute/adversarial.py:_obs.name: _obs_by_subject
function:app/compute/adversarial.py:_obs.summary: Groups raw observations by subject from a dataframe using role mappings, for independent recomputation.

function:app/compute/adversarial.py:_nca.name: _nca_recompute
function:app/compute/adversarial.py:_nca.summary: Recomputes Cmax and an AUC plausibility band directly from raw observations to detect disagreement with reported NCA values.

function:app/compute/adversarial.py:revi.name: review
function:app/compute/adversarial.py:revi.summary: Top-level adversarial review: runs internal checks plus raw-data recomputation against a state dump and goal, returning severity-sorted findings.

file:app/compute/bioequivalence.py.name: bioequivalence.py
file:app/compute/bioequivalence.py.summary: Average bioequivalence assessment computing geometric mean ratios and 90% confidence intervals (paired crossover and parallel designs) against the 80-125% acceptance window.

function:app/compute/bioequivalence.py:b.name: be_one_parameter
function:app/compute/bioequivalence.py:b.summary: Assesses bioequivalence for a single PK parameter, dispatching to paired or parallel computation.

function:app/compute/bioequivalence.py:_.name: _be_paired
function:app/compute/bioequivalence.py:_.summary: Paired (crossover) bioequivalence: log-scale mean difference, standard error, and CI on test/reference exposures.

function:app/compute/bioequivalence.py:_.name: _be_parallel
function:app/compute/bioequivalence.py:_.summary: Parallel-design bioequivalence: two-group log-scale comparison with pooled variance and CI.

function:app/compute/bioequivalence.py:a.name: assess_bioequivalence
function:app/compute/bioequivalence.py:a.summary: Assesses bioequivalence across all common PK parameters, blocking overall BE if any parameter fails.

file:app/compute/compartmental.py.name: compartmental.py
file:app/compute/compartmental.py.summary: Closed-form one- and two-compartment oral PK models (single-dose and steady-state) plus least-squares fitting of individual subjects and dose-grouped cohorts.

function:app/compute/compartmental.py:co.name: conc_1cmt_oral
function:app/compute/compartmental.py:co.summary: Closed-form 1-compartment oral concentration over time given ka, CL, V.

function:app/compute/compartmental.py:co.name: conc_2cmt_oral
function:app/compute/compartmental.py:co.summary: Closed-form 2-compartment oral concentration solving the biexponential disposition from ka, CL, V1, Q, V2.

function:app/compute/compartmental.py:co.name: conc_1cmt_oral_ss
function:app/compute/compartmental.py:co.summary: Steady-state 1-compartment oral concentration over a dosing interval via superposition.

function:app/compute/compartmental.py:co.name: conc_2cmt_oral_ss
function:app/compute/compartmental.py:co.summary: Steady-state 2-compartment oral concentration over a dosing interval via superposition accumulation.

function:app/compute/compartmental.py:_i.name: _initial_estimates
function:app/compute/compartmental.py:_i.summary: Derives initial parameter guesses (ka, CL, V) from observed time-concentration data before fitting.

function:app/compute/compartmental.py:fi.name: fit_one_subject
function:app/compute/compartmental.py:fi.summary: Fits a single subject's concentration-time profile to a chosen compartmental model by nonlinear least squares, returning parameters and fit metrics.

function:app/compute/compartmental.py:fi.name: fit_compartmental
function:app/compute/compartmental.py:fi.summary: Fits compartmental models across subjects grouped by dose, selecting the best model per group.

function:app/compute/compartmental.py:fi.name: fit_compartmental_ss
function:app/compute/compartmental.py:fi.summary: Fits steady-state compartmental models to extracted steady-state interval profiles.

file:app/compute/diagnostics.py.name: diagnostics.py
file:app/compute/diagnostics.py.summary: Pure, deterministic model goodness-of-fit residual diagnostics (log-scale weighted residuals and NPDE) built on the structural PK/PK-PD simulator.

function:app/compute/diagnostics.py:fit_.name: fit_residuals
function:app/compute/diagnostics.py:fit_.summary: Computes population and individual weighted residuals (log scale) by pairing observations with typical and individual predictions, returning summary statistics.

function:app/compute/diagnostics.py:npde.name: npde
function:app/compute/diagnostics.py:npde.summary: Computes normalized prediction distribution errors by simulating each observation under the population IIV model with a fixed seed.

file:app/compute/dose_proportionality.py.name: dose_proportionality.py
file:app/compute/dose_proportionality.py.summary: Power-model dose-proportionality assessment: fits log(exposure) vs log(dose), computing the slope, confidence interval, and a critical-region acceptance test.

function:app/compute/dose_proportionalit.name: power_model
function:app/compute/dose_proportionalit.summary: Fits the power model of exposure vs dose, returning slope, standard error, CI, and the proportionality critical-region decision.

function:app/compute/dose_proportionalit.name: assess_dose_proportionality
function:app/compute/dose_proportionalit.summary: Aggregates power-model dose-proportionality assessments across all PK parameters.

file:app/compute/dose_sweep.py.name: dose_sweep.py
file:app/compute/dose_sweep.py.summary: Dose-sweep simulator computing steady-state exposure metrics (AUC, Cmax, Cavg, trough) across a range of doses for a structural PK model.

function:app/compute/dose_sweep.py:_inte.name: _interval_metrics
function:app/compute/dose_sweep.py:_inte.summary: Computes per-interval exposure metrics (AUC over tau, Cmax, Cavg, trough) from a simulated concentration profile.

function:app/compute/dose_sweep.py:dose_.name: dose_sweep
function:app/compute/dose_sweep.py:dose_.summary: Sweeps a model across multiple doses, simulating each to steady state and returning interval exposure metrics and the response envelope.

file:app/compute/dosing.py.name: dosing.py
file:app/compute/dosing.py.summary: Dosing-record utilities: expands NONMEM-style dose events (II/ADDL), detects multiple-dose regimens, and extracts steady-state dosing intervals from concentration records.

function:app/compute/dosing.py:_dose_tim.name: _dose_times
function:app/compute/dosing.py:_dose_tim.summary: Expands a dose row's interval (II) and additional-doses (ADDL) fields into explicit dose times.

function:app/compute/dosing.py:is_multip.name: is_multiple_dose
function:app/compute/dosing.py:is_multip.summary: Detects whether a record set represents a multiple-dose regimen.

function:app/compute/dosing.py:extract_s.name: extract_ss_intervals
function:app/compute/dosing.py:extract_s.summary: Extracts per-subject steady-state dosing intervals (time-after-dose profiles) from concentration records for steady-state NCA and fitting.

class:app/compute/dosing.py:SSProfile.name: SSProfile
class:app/compute/dosing.py:SSProfile.summary: Dataclass describing one subject's steady-state interval: time-after-dose, concentrations, dose, tau, number of doses, and trough source.

file:app/compute/forecast.py.name: forecast.py
file:app/compute/forecast.py.summary: Bayesian dose forecasting: MAP-estimates individual parameters from measured levels, simulates steady-state exposure, and optimizes the dose to hit a target metric.

function:app/compute/forecast.py:_optimi.name: _optimize_dose
function:app/compute/forecast.py:_optimi.summary: Optimizes the dose to achieve a target steady-state exposure metric for given individual parameters.

function:app/compute/forecast.py:forecas.name: forecast
function:app/compute/forecast.py:forecas.summary: End-to-end TDM forecast: derives individual PK via MAP from measured levels (or population typical), simulates, and optionally optimizes dose to target.

file:app/compute/nca_ss.py.name: nca_ss.py
file:app/compute/nca_ss.py.summary: Steady-state non-compartmental analysis computing per-subject AUC over tau, Cmax/Cmin, CL/F, and accumulation, then summarizing by dose.

function:app/compute/nca_ss.py:nca_ss_su.name: nca_ss_subject
function:app/compute/nca_ss.py:nca_ss_su.summary: Computes steady-state NCA parameters for one subject's dosing interval (AUCtau, Cmax, Cmin, Cavg, CL/F).

function:app/compute/nca_ss.py:run_nca_s.name: run_nca_ss
function:app/compute/nca_ss.py:run_nca_s.summary: Runs steady-state NCA over all subject profiles and summarizes results grouped by dose.

file:app/compute/nca.py.name: nca.py
file:app/compute/nca.py.summary: Single-dose non-compartmental analysis: lambda_z selection, AUC by trapezoid, Cmax/Tmax, CL/F, half-life, percent extrapolation, plus geometric-mean dose summaries.

function:app/compute/nca.py:_auc_interva.name: _auc_intervals
function:app/compute/nca.py:_auc_interva.summary: Computes interval AUCs by the linear trapezoidal rule over a concentration-time profile.

function:app/compute/nca.py:_best_lambda.name: _best_lambda_z
function:app/compute/nca.py:_best_lambda.summary: Selects the terminal elimination rate (lambda_z) by maximizing adjusted R^2 over candidate terminal windows.

function:app/compute/nca.py:refit_lambda.name: refit_lambda_z_manual
function:app/compute/nca.py:refit_lambda.summary: Refits lambda_z from a user-selected set of terminal points for manual NCA override.

function:app/compute/nca.py:nca_subject.name: nca_subject
function:app/compute/nca.py:nca_subject.summary: Computes full NCA parameters for one subject (Cmax, Tmax, AUClast, AUCinf, CL/F, Vz/F, t-half, % extrapolated) for IV or oral dosing.

function:app/compute/nca.py:summarize_by.name: summarize_by_dose
function:app/compute/nca.py:summarize_by.summary: Aggregates per-subject NCA parameters into geometric-mean and geometric-CV summaries grouped by dose.

function:app/compute/nca.py:run_nca.name: run_nca
function:app/compute/nca.py:run_nca.summary: Runs single-dose NCA across all subjects from records and dose mapping, returning per-subject and by-dose summaries.

file:app/compute/nlme.py.name: nlme.py
file:app/compute/nlme.py.summary: True nonlinear mixed-effects estimation for the PK model library: FOCE-I (Laplace conditional EBEs) and SAEM estimators sharing a lognormal IIV and residual-error model, plus stepwise covariate modeling, parameter uncertainty, and MAP estimation.

function:app/compute/nlme.py:_build_cov_.name: _build_cov_effects
function:app/compute/nlme.py:_build_cov_.summary: Builds covariate-effect descriptors from a model specification and subject covariates.

function:app/compute/nlme.py:_ind_obj.name: _ind_obj
function:app/compute/nlme.py:_ind_obj.summary: Individual conditional objective function (joint log-likelihood of data and random effects) minimized to find a subject's EBE.

function:app/compute/nlme.py:_numeric_he.name: _numeric_hessian
function:app/compute/nlme.py:_numeric_he.summary: Finite-difference numeric Hessian of an objective used for the Laplace approximation and uncertainty.

function:app/compute/nlme.py:_conditiona.name: _conditional_mode
function:app/compute/nlme.py:_conditiona.summary: Finds a subject's empirical Bayes estimate by minimizing the individual conditional objective.

function:app/compute/nlme.py:_laplace_su.name: _laplace_subject
function:app/compute/nlme.py:_laplace_su.summary: Laplace-approximated marginal likelihood contribution for one subject, integrating out random effects.

function:app/compute/nlme.py:_population.name: _population_ofv
function:app/compute/nlme.py:_population.summary: Population objective function value summed over all subjects' Laplace contributions.

function:app/compute/nlme.py:_initial_th.name: _initial_theta
function:app/compute/nlme.py:_initial_th.summary: Derives initial fixed-effect (theta) estimates from subject data before population fitting.

function:app/compute/nlme.py:_parameter_.name: _parameter_uncertainty
function:app/compute/nlme.py:_parameter_.summary: Estimates parameter standard errors and RSEs from the objective Hessian, with condition-number reporting.

function:app/compute/nlme.py:_assemble.name: _assemble
function:app/compute/nlme.py:_assemble.summary: Assembles the final NLME result object (theta, omega, sigma, OFV, EBEs, covariate records, uncertainty) from a completed fit.

function:app/compute/nlme.py:focei_fit.name: focei_fit
function:app/compute/nlme.py:focei_fit.summary: FOCE-I population fit: iteratively optimizes fixed effects and variance components using per-subject Laplace EBEs, returning estimates, shrinkage, and uncertainty.

function:app/compute/nlme.py:_saem_estep.name: _saem_estep
function:app/compute/nlme.py:_saem_estep.summary: SAEM expectation step: MCMC sampling of subject random effects given current parameters.

function:app/compute/nlme.py:_saem_updat.name: _saem_update_theta
function:app/compute/nlme.py:_saem_updat.summary: SAEM maximization step updating fixed effects and variance components from stochastic-approximation sufficient statistics.

function:app/compute/nlme.py:saem_fit.name: saem_fit
function:app/compute/nlme.py:saem_fit.summary: SAEM population fit: stochastic-approximation EM alternating MCMC E-step and parameter M-step, deterministic for a fixed seed.

function:app/compute/nlme.py:population_.name: population_fit
function:app/compute/nlme.py:population_.summary: Dispatches a population fit to FOCE-I or SAEM by method and returns the unified result.

function:app/compute/nlme.py:_warm_init.name: _warm_init
function:app/compute/nlme.py:_warm_init.summary: Warm-starts a candidate covariate fit from a parent result's estimates to accelerate SCM.

function:app/compute/nlme.py:_fit_batch.name: _fit_batch
function:app/compute/nlme.py:_fit_batch.summary: Fits a batch of candidate models, optionally across a process pool, for stepwise covariate modeling.

function:app/compute/nlme.py:scm.name: scm
function:app/compute/nlme.py:scm.summary: Stepwise covariate modeling: forward-selection then backward-elimination of covariate effects by OFV change, with warm-start and optional parallelism.

function:app/compute/nlme.py:map_estimat.name: map_estimate
function:app/compute/nlme.py:map_estimat.summary: Maximum a posteriori estimation of an individual's random effects from observed concentrations and a population prior (used by TDM forecasting).

class:app/compute/nlme.py:_CovEffect.name: _CovEffect
class:app/compute/nlme.py:_CovEffect.summary: Covariate-effect model: holds parameter/covariate linkage, centering, and a multiplier factor with describe/key helpers.

class:app/compute/nlme.py:_PopSpec.name: _PopSpec
class:app/compute/nlme.py:_PopSpec.summary: Population-model specification bundling structural model, IIV parameters, error model, and covariate effects for the estimators.

class:app/compute/nlme.py:_Subject.name: _Subject
class:app/compute/nlme.py:_Subject.summary: Internal per-subject record holding doses, observation times/concentrations, weight, and covariates for population fitting.

file:app/compute/nmexport.py.name: nmexport.py
file:app/compute/nmexport.py.summary: Generates NONMEM control streams and mrgsolve C++ model code from a fitted NLME result, mapping structural models to ADVAN/TRANS and translating IIV, residual error, and covariate effects.

function:app/compute/nmexport.py:build_n.name: build_nonmem
function:app/compute/nmexport.py:build_n.summary: Builds a NONMEM control stream from an NLME fit, selecting the ADVAN/TRANS subroutine and emitting $PK/$THETA/$OMEGA/$SIGMA with covariate and lag terms.

function:app/compute/nmexport.py:build_m.name: build_mrgsolve
function:app/compute/nmexport.py:build_m.summary: Builds an mrgsolve C++ model (ODE plus parameter/IIV/error blocks) from an NLME fit.

file:app/compute/pk_fit.py.name: pk_fit.py
file:app/compute/pk_fit.py.summary: Individual (naive-pooled / two-stage) fitting of structural PK and PK-PD models by nonlinear least squares, with Jacobian-based RSEs, dataset-level fitting, and AIC model comparison.

function:app/compute/pk_fit.py:rse_from_.name: rse_from_jacobian
function:app/compute/pk_fit.py:rse_from_.summary: Derives parameter relative standard errors from the least-squares Jacobian and residual sum of squares.

function:app/compute/pk_fit.py:fit_subje.name: fit_subject_model
function:app/compute/pk_fit.py:fit_subje.summary: Fits one subject's concentration data to a structural PK model by nonlinear least squares, returning parameters, RSEs, and fit metrics.

function:app/compute/pk_fit.py:_pkpd_ini.name: _pkpd_init
function:app/compute/pk_fit.py:_pkpd_ini.summary: Builds initial parameter guesses for a joint PK-PD fit from PK and PD observations.

function:app/compute/pk_fit.py:fit_subje.name: fit_subject_pkpd
function:app/compute/pk_fit.py:fit_subje.summary: Fits a subject's joint PK-PD model (concentration and effect endpoints) by nonlinear least squares.

function:app/compute/pk_fit.py:fit_pk_da.name: fit_pk_dataset
function:app/compute/pk_fit.py:fit_pk_da.summary: Fits a structural model to every subject in a dataset and summarizes parameters via two-stage statistics.

function:app/compute/pk_fit.py:compare_m.name: compare_models
function:app/compute/pk_fit.py:compare_m.summary: Ranks candidate structural models by AIC across the subject set.

file:app/compute/pk_models.py.name: pk_models.py
file:app/compute/pk_models.py.summary: Structural PK / PK-PD model library (18 models ported from the PopPK Workbench): each model is a pure-Python ODE system with allometric scaling, dosing-compartment conventions, and a concentration map, exposed through a registry.

function:app/compute/pk_models.py:list_m.name: list_models
function:app/compute/pk_models.py:list_m.summary: Returns the registry of available structural PK/PK-PD models.

function:app/compute/pk_models.py:get_mo.name: get_model
function:app/compute/pk_models.py:get_mo.summary: Looks up a structural model definition by its key.

class:app/compute/pk_models.py:PKModel.name: PKModel
class:app/compute/pk_models.py:PKModel.summary: Dataclass describing one structural model: key, label, group, IV/PD flags, parameters, defaults, compartment layout, ODE right-hand side, concentration map, init state, effect function, and lag parameter.

file:app/compute/pk_simulate.py.name: pk_simulate.py
file:app/compute/pk_simulate.py.summary: Numerical simulator integrating the structural PK/PK-PD ODE models over dose events and observation times, with allometric parameter scaling and a steady-state time-course helper.

function:app/compute/pk_simulate.py:scal.name: scale_params
function:app/compute/pk_simulate.py:scal.summary: Applies allometric weight scaling to a model's parameters before simulation.

function:app/compute/pk_simulate.py:simu.name: simulate
function:app/compute/pk_simulate.py:simu.summary: Integrates a structural model's ODE system across dose events and returns predicted concentrations at the requested observation times.

function:app/compute/pk_simulate.py:simu.name: simulate_timecourse
function:app/compute/pk_simulate.py:simu.summary: Simulates a dense steady-state concentration time-course for a multiple-dose regimen.

file:app/compute/poppk.py.name: poppk.py
file:app/compute/poppk.py.summary: Two-stage population summary statistics: geometric-mean typical values and between-subject IIV from individual fits, plus log-linear covariate-effect estimation.

function:app/compute/poppk.py:two_stage_.name: two_stage_summary
function:app/compute/poppk.py:two_stage_.summary: Summarizes individual-fit parameters into geometric-mean typical values and IIV (geometric CV) per parameter.

function:app/compute/poppk.py:covariate_.name: covariate_effect
function:app/compute/poppk.py:covariate_.summary: Estimates a log-linear covariate effect on a parameter from matched individual-fit and covariate values.

file:app/compute/vpc.py.name: vpc.py
file:app/compute/vpc.py.summary: Pure, deterministic observed-vs-predicted goodness-of-fit and visual predictive check (VPC, including prediction-corrected pcVPC) built on the structural PK/PK-PD simulator.

function:app/compute/vpc.py:obs_vs_pred.name: obs_vs_pred
function:app/compute/vpc.py:obs_vs_pred.summary: Pairs every positive observation with its individual prediction (IPRED) for goodness-of-fit plots and metrics.

function:app/compute/vpc.py:_gof_log.name: _gof_log
function:app/compute/vpc.py:_gof_log.summary: Computes log-scale goodness-of-fit metrics between observed and individually predicted concentrations.

function:app/compute/vpc.py:vpc_band.name: vpc_band
function:app/compute/vpc.py:vpc_band.summary: Builds simulation-based VPC percentile bands by sampling the population IIV model on a time grid with a fixed seed.

function:app/compute/vpc.py:pcvpc.name: pcvpc
function:app/compute/vpc.py:pcvpc.summary: Computes a prediction-corrected VPC: bins observations and simulations and normalizes by typical prediction to remove dosing/covariate heterogeneity.

file:app/core/store.py.name: store.py
file:app/core/store.py.summary: SQLite persistence layer for sessions and their audit trails: SessionStore saves and loads session state plus serialized audit chains keyed by session id.

class:app/core/store.py:SessionStore.name: SessionStore
class:app/core/store.py:SessionStore.summary: SQLite repository persisting session records (owner, state, audit list) with save, load_all, and get accessors used by the orchestrator on startup and after each mutation.

file:app/db/__init__.py.name: __init__.py
file:app/db/__init__.py.summary: Empty package marker for the app.db package.

file:app/main.py.name: main.py
file:app/main.py.summary: FastAPI application entry point wiring all HTTP routes for PharmAgent: sessions, dataset upload, chat, workflows, every PK/NCA/BE/NLME/SCM analysis, async jobs, exports (CSV/control/CDISC), skills, adversarial review, reports, and the SPA frontend mount, with bearer auth and a request-context/error envelope.

function:app/main.py:request_context.name: request_context
function:app/main.py:request_context.summary: ASGI middleware that attaches a request id, logs structured request/response context, and normalizes errors into the standard envelope.

function:app/main.py:current_owner.name: current_owner
function:app/main.py:current_owner.summary: Auth dependency that resolves the calling owner from the bearer Authorization header, enforcing the configured API token.

function:app/main.py:upload_for_session.name: upload_for_session
function:app/main.py:upload_for_session.summary: Endpoint that accepts a dataset file upload for a session, persists it within the allowed data directory, and registers it on the session state.

function:app/main.py:run_nlme.name: run_nlme
function:app/main.py:run_nlme.summary: Endpoint that submits a population NLME fit as a background job and returns its job id for polling.

function:app/main.py:export_cdisc.name: export_cdisc
function:app/main.py:export_cdisc.summary: Endpoint that builds and streams the CDISC ADaM submission zip (ADPC/ADPP/define.xml) for a session.

function:app/main.py:run_skill.name: run_skill
function:app/main.py:run_skill.summary: Endpoint that replays a previously captured skill workflow against a session for the calling owner.

file:app/tools/__init__.py.name: __init__.py
file:app/tools/__init__.py.summary: Empty package marker for the app.tools package containing all agent tool modules.

file:app/tools/base.py.name: base.py
file:app/tools/base.py.summary: Tool framework foundation: ToolContext and ToolResult value objects, the Tool descriptor (with to_anthropic schema export), and a ToolRegistry that registers, looks up, scopes per-agent, and executes tools.

class:app/tools/base.py:Tool.name: Tool
class:app/tools/base.py:Tool.summary: Descriptor for a single callable tool (name, description, input schema, handler, agent scope) with to_anthropic to expose it to the LLM.

class:app/tools/base.py:ToolRegistry.name: ToolRegistry
class:app/tools/base.py:ToolRegistry.summary: Central registry of all tools supporting register, get, names, per-agent filtering (for_agent), and execute, which invokes a tool handler against a PharmState/context.

file:app/tools/be_tools.py.name: be_tools.py
file:app/tools/be_tools.py.summary: Bioequivalence Agent tools that derive test/reference geometric mean ratios and 90% confidence intervals from NCA exposures, exposed as the module's TOOLS for the registry.

function:app/tools/be_tools.py:run_bioeq.name: run_bioequivalence
function:app/tools/be_tools.py:run_bioeq.summary: Computes the test-vs-reference GMR and 90% CI for PK exposures across treatment arms and writes the bioequivalence result to PharmState.

file:app/tools/builtins.py.name: builtins.py
file:app/tools/builtins.py.summary: Assembles the default ToolRegistry by collecting the TOOLS lists from every tool module (data, NCA, BE, dose-proportionality, compartmental, popPK, PK model, QC, report, regulatory, review).

function:app/tools/builtins.py:default_r.name: default_registry
function:app/tools/builtins.py:default_r.summary: Builds and returns a ToolRegistry populated with all tools from the bundled tool modules; the single wiring point used by the orchestrator.

file:app/tools/compartmental_tools.py.name: compartmental_tools.py
file:app/tools/compartmental_tools.py.summary: Compartmental Agent tools that fit 1- and 2-compartment oral PK models per subject and write a compacted comparison to PharmState, exposed via the module TOOLS.

function:app/tools/compartmental_tools.p.name: fit_compartmental_models
function:app/tools/compartmental_tools.p.summary: Fits one- and two-compartment oral models per subject, compares them, and stores a compact per-model summary (optionally steady-state) in PharmState.

file:app/tools/data_tools.py.name: data_tools.py
file:app/tools/data_tools.py.summary: Data Manager tools to load, profile, validate (CDISC), and visualize PK datasets, with path-sandboxing and role detection; exposed as TOOLS for the registry.

function:app/tools/data_tools.py:load_da.name: load_dataset
function:app/tools/data_tools.py:load_da.summary: Loads a dataset from a sandbox-validated path into PharmState, recording detected column roles and metadata.

function:app/tools/data_tools.py:profile.name: profile_pk_dataset
function:app/tools/data_tools.py:profile.summary: Profiles a loaded PK dataset (subjects, records, time/concentration ranges, role coverage) and stores the profile summary on PharmState.

function:app/tools/data_tools.py:validat.name: validate_cdisc
function:app/tools/data_tools.py:validat.summary: Checks a dataset against CDISC-style structural expectations and records validation findings on PharmState.

function:app/tools/data_tools.py:generat.name: generate_spaghetti_plot
function:app/tools/data_tools.py:generat.summary: Generates a per-subject concentration-vs-time spaghetti plot for the loaded dataset and stores the rendered figure on PharmState.

file:app/tools/dp_tools.py.name: dp_tools.py
file:app/tools/dp_tools.py.summary: Dose-Proportionality Agent tool that assesses dose proportionality via the power model over NCA exposures, exposed as the module TOOLS.

function:app/tools/dp_tools.py:run_dose_.name: run_dose_proportionality
function:app/tools/dp_tools.py:run_dose_.summary: Fits the power model (log exposure vs log dose) across dose levels to assess dose proportionality and writes the result to PharmState.

file:app/tools/nca_tools.py.name: nca_tools.py
file:app/tools/nca_tools.py.summary: NCA Agent tool computing non-compartmental PK parameters (Cmax, Tmax, AUC, half-life, lambda-z, etc.) including steady-state handling, exposed as the module TOOLS.

function:app/tools/nca_tools.py:compute_.name: compute_nca
function:app/tools/nca_tools.py:compute_.summary: Computes the full non-compartmental parameter set per subject (exposure, peak, terminal-slope, half-life and steady-state metrics) and stores the NCA summary on PharmState.

file:app/tools/pkmodel_tools.py.name: pkmodel_tools.py
file:app/tools/pkmodel_tools.py.summary: Modeler Agent tool suite: fits/compares the structural PK model library, simulates profiles, runs VPC, true NLME, stepwise covariate modeling (SCM), MAP/TDM forecasting, diagnostics, and dose sweeps over PharmState.

function:app/tools/pkmodel_tools.py:_bui.name: _build_subjects
function:app/tools/pkmodel_tools.py:_bui.summary: Transforms the source dataframe into per-subject dosing/observation records (optionally flagging BLQ) used as the common input for all PK model fitting routines.

function:app/tools/pkmodel_tools.py:fit_.name: fit_pk_model
function:app/tools/pkmodel_tools.py:fit_.summary: Fits and compares candidate structural PK models from the library, selecting a best fit and storing parameters, fit metrics, and RSEs on PharmState.

function:app/tools/pkmodel_tools.py:simu.name: simulate_pk_profile
function:app/tools/pkmodel_tools.py:simu.summary: Simulates concentration-time profiles for a chosen model and dosing regimen and stores the simulated curves on PharmState.

function:app/tools/pkmodel_tools.py:run_.name: run_vpc
function:app/tools/pkmodel_tools.py:run_.summary: Runs a (prediction-corrected) visual predictive check by simulating replicate datasets and summarizing observed-vs-predicted percentiles into PharmState.

function:app/tools/pkmodel_tools.py:run_.name: run_nlme
function:app/tools/pkmodel_tools.py:run_.summary: Fits a true nonlinear mixed-effects population model (FOCE-I/SAEM) over the assembled subjects and stores thetas, omega/shrinkage, and sigma on PharmState.

function:app/tools/pkmodel_tools.py:run_.name: run_scm
function:app/tools/pkmodel_tools.py:run_.summary: Performs stepwise covariate modeling over candidate covariate-parameter relationships and records the selected covariate model on PharmState.

function:app/tools/pkmodel_tools.py:fore.name: forecast_map
function:app/tools/pkmodel_tools.py:fore.summary: Produces a MAP/TDM Bayesian individual forecast from prior population parameters and observed levels, storing the forecast on PharmState.

function:app/tools/pkmodel_tools.py:run_.name: run_diagnostics
function:app/tools/pkmodel_tools.py:run_.summary: Computes residual/goodness-of-fit diagnostics for the last fitted model and stores the diagnostic summary on PharmState.

function:app/tools/pkmodel_tools.py:run_.name: run_dose_sweep
function:app/tools/pkmodel_tools.py:run_.summary: Sweeps a range of doses through the fitted model to project exposure metrics and stores the dose-sweep table on PharmState.

file:app/config.py.name: config.py
file:app/config.py.summary: Application configuration via a pydantic Settings model: Anthropic API key/model, mock-LLM toggle, data and DB paths, auth token, CORS origins, and org branding. Centralizes runtime env wiring for the backend.

class:app/config.py:Settings.name: Settings
class:app/config.py:Settings.summary: pydantic settings object holding all backend runtime config; llm_is_mock decides mock vs real LLM and allowed_data_dirs returns the directory allowlist used to sandbox dataset file access.

file:app/core/__init__.py.name: __init__.py
file:app/core/__init__.py.summary: Empty package marker for the app.core package, which holds the orchestrator, audit chain, persistence, and supporting services.

file:app/core/audit.py.name: audit.py
file:app/core/audit.py.summary: Tamper-evident hash-chain audit trail: each AuditEntry links to the previous via SHA-256 over its inputs/outputs, and AuditChain appends, verifies, and (de)serializes the chain for Part-11-style provenance.

class:app/core/audit.py:AuditEntry.name: AuditEntry
class:app/core/audit.py:AuditEntry.summary: Single immutable audit record (agent, tool, action, input/output hashes, actor, reason); compute_hash derives the chained entry hash and to_dict serializes it.

class:app/core/audit.py:AuditChain.name: AuditChain
class:app/core/audit.py:AuditChain.summary: Ordered chain of AuditEntry records with append (links new entries to the head hash), verify (re-derives every hash to detect tampering), and to_list/from_list round-tripping.

file:app/core/cdisc.py.name: cdisc.py
file:app/core/cdisc.py.summary: Builds CDISC ADaM-style regulatory deliverables from PharmState: ADPC (concentration records), ADPP (NCA parameters), and a define.xml metadata document, zipped together by build_package.

function:app/core/cdisc.py:build_adpp.name: build_adpp
function:app/core/cdisc.py:build_adpp.summary: Constructs ADPP (PK parameter) rows from the stored NCA summary, mapping internal parameter keys to CDISC PARAMCD/PARAM labels.

function:app/core/cdisc.py:build_adpc.name: build_adpc
function:app/core/cdisc.py:build_adpc.summary: Constructs ADPC (concentration) rows from the source dataframe using detected ID/time/DV roles, coercing numerics and skipping missing values.

function:app/core/cdisc.py:build_define_.name: build_define_xml
function:app/core/cdisc.py:build_define_.summary: Generates a define.xml metadata document describing each emitted dataset and its variables, with XML-escaped study and column metadata.

function:app/core/cdisc.py:build_package.name: build_package
function:app/core/cdisc.py:build_package.summary: Assembles the full CDISC submission package: builds ADPP/ADPC/define.xml and writes them as CSV/XML into a single in-memory zip archive.

file:app/core/exporters.py.name: exporters.py
file:app/core/exporters.py.summary: CSV exporters for every analysis result held in PharmState (NCA, BE, dose-proportionality, PK model, dose sweep, VPC, NLME) plus NONMEM/mrgsolve control-file exporters, with availability discovery helpers.

function:app/core/exporters.py:_pk_model.name: _pk_model
function:app/core/exporters.py:_pk_model.summary: Flattens stored PK model-fit results (best model parameters, fit metrics, relative standard errors) into CSV rows.

function:app/core/exporters.py:_nlme.name: _nlme
function:app/core/exporters.py:_nlme.summary: Serializes NLME population-fit output (thetas with RSE, omega/shrinkage, sigma) into a tidy CSV parameter table.

function:app/core/exporters.py:available.name: available
function:app/core/exporters.py:available.summary: Inspects PharmState and returns the list of analysis-result kinds that currently have data available for CSV export.

function:app/core/exporters.py:export_cs.name: export_csv
function:app/core/exporters.py:export_cs.summary: Dispatches to the matching per-kind CSV formatter, raising ValueError for unknown export kinds.

function:app/core/exporters.py:export_co.name: export_control
function:app/core/exporters.py:export_co.summary: Produces NONMEM/mrgsolve control-file text for the requested kind from stored NLME results, validating availability before emitting.

file:app/core/jobs.py.name: jobs.py
file:app/core/jobs.py.summary: Thread-pool background job manager for long-running tools (NLME, SCM): submit returns a job id, work runs off-request, and get polls status with bounded retention of finished jobs.

class:app/core/jobs.py:JobManager.name: JobManager
class:app/core/jobs.py:JobManager.summary: Manages async submission and polling of background tool runs via a thread pool, recording start/finish timestamps, results, and errors under a lock.

file:app/core/llm.py.name: llm.py
file:app/core/llm.py.summary: LLM client abstraction with an LLM protocol, a keyless MockLLM (regex/heuristic routing for tests), a RealLLM backed by the Anthropic SDK, and a get_llm factory that picks mock vs real from settings.

class:app/core/llm.py:MockLLM.name: MockLLM
class:app/core/llm.py:MockLLM.summary: Deterministic keyless LLM stand-in that classifies intent and selects tools via regex and keyword heuristics, enabling tests and offline runs.

class:app/core/llm.py:RealLLM.name: RealLLM
class:app/core/llm.py:RealLLM.summary: Anthropic-backed LLM client that performs intent classification and tool selection through the messages API using each tool's to_anthropic schema.

file:app/core/logging_config.py.name: logging_config.py
file:app/core/logging_config.py.summary: Structured JSON logging setup with a dependency-free JsonFormatter emitting one JSON line per record, plus configure_logging to install it from a level or env var.

class:app/core/logging_config.py:JsonFor.name: JsonFormatter
class:app/core/logging_config.py:JsonFor.summary: logging.Formatter that renders each record as a single JSON object including timestamp, level, message, extra fields, and formatted exceptions.

file:app/core/orchestrator.py.name: orchestrator.py
file:app/core/orchestrator.py.summary: Central orchestrator: owns sessions, routes chat to agents, executes PK/tool runs with per-session locking, runs the adversarial review loop and workflows, captures/replays skills, and persists state plus the audit chain.

class:app/core/orchestrator.py:Session.name: Session
class:app/core/orchestrator.py:Session.summary: Dataclass bundling a session's PharmState, tool context, audit chain, chat history, pending review, params, owner, and command log.

class:app/core/orchestrator.py:Orchestra.name: Orchestrator
class:app/core/orchestrator.py:Orchestra.summary: Coordinates the whole backend: session lifecycle, ownership checks, chat routing, tool/PK execution, review loops, skill capture/replay, role overrides, and multi-step workflow advancement with persistence and audit verification.

file:app/core/pharmstate.py.name: pharmstate.py
file:app/core/pharmstate.py.summary: Defines PharmState, the typed pydantic communication bus shared across agents (datasets, roles, and every analysis result), plus apply_writes which validates and merges agent-produced field writes.

class:app/core/pharmstate.py:PharmState.name: PharmState
class:app/core/pharmstate.py:PharmState.summary: Central typed pydantic model carrying dataset metadata, column roles, and all analysis outputs (NCA, BE, PK model, NLME, VPC, etc.) passed between agents.

function:app/core/pharmstate.py:apply_wr.name: apply_writes
function:app/core/pharmstate.py:apply_wr.summary: Validates and applies a set of agent-produced field writes onto a PharmState, rejecting writes to undeclared fields to keep the shared bus type-safe.

file:app/core/provenance.py.name: provenance.py
file:app/core/provenance.py.summary: Captures run provenance for reproducibility: key package versions, platform info, git SHA, app version, and SHA-256 content hashes of input files.

function:app/core/provenance.py:collect_.name: collect_provenance
function:app/core/provenance.py:collect_.summary: Assembles a provenance record combining app version, dependency versions, platform details, and the current git commit SHA.

function:app/core/provenance.py:file_sha.name: file_sha256
function:app/core/provenance.py:file_sha.summary: Computes the SHA-256 hash of a file's contents in chunks for dataset content fingerprinting.

file:app/core/schema_extractor.py.name: schema_extractor.py
file:app/core/schema_extractor.py.summary: Privacy boundary that turns a raw dataframe into a non-identifying schema: detects PK column roles and emits per-column type and numeric-summary metadata without leaking row-level data.

function:app/core/schema_extractor.py:de.name: detect_roles
function:app/core/schema_extractor.py:de.summary: Heuristically maps dataframe columns to PK roles (subject ID, time, concentration/DV, dose, etc.) from their names.

function:app/core/schema_extractor.py:ex.name: extract_schema
function:app/core/schema_extractor.py:ex.summary: Builds the privacy-safe dataset schema: per-column dtype, role assignment, and numeric summaries, packaged with a dataset id for downstream agents.

file:app/core/skills.py.name: skills.py
file:app/core/skills.py.summary: Captured, replayable analysis workflows: distill_steps condenses a session command log into reusable steps, the Skill model serializes to dict/markdown, and SkillStore persists skills in SQLite.

function:app/core/skills.py:distill_step.name: distill_steps
function:app/core/skills.py:distill_step.summary: Condenses a session's raw command log into an ordered list of replayable skill steps, dropping non-reproducible noise.

class:app/core/skills.py:Skill.name: Skill
class:app/core/skills.py:Skill.summary: Represents a named, captured analysis workflow with ordered steps; serializes to dict and human-readable markdown.

class:app/core/skills.py:SkillStore.name: SkillStore
class:app/core/skills.py:SkillStore.summary: SQLite-backed repository for captured skills with save, get, list, and delete operations.

file:app/tools/poppk_tools.py.name: poppk_tools.py
file:app/tools/poppk_tools.py.summary: PopPK agent tool exposing a deterministic two-stage (STS) population summary and covariate screen, deriving typical values and IIV from individual compartmental or NCA fits rather than a true NLME fit.

function:app/tools/poppk_tools.py:run_po.name: run_poppk
function:app/tools/poppk_tools.py:run_po.summary: Tool entrypoint that builds per-subject parameter rows, computes two-stage typical value and geometric-CV IIV, and screens covariate relationships, returning a ToolResult summary.

function:app/tools/poppk_tools.py:_covar.name: _covariate_by_subject
function:app/tools/poppk_tools.py:_covar.summary: Maps a detected covariate column to a per-subject numeric value by grouping the dataset on the subject role and taking each subject's first usable covariate observation.

function:app/tools/poppk_tools.py:_from_.name: _from_compartmental
function:app/tools/poppk_tools.py:_from_.summary: Extracts per-subject CL/V/KA parameter rows from a previously stored compartmental fit in state for use as the two-stage input.

file:app/tools/qc_tools.py.name: qc_tools.py
file:app/tools/qc_tools.py.summary: Quality-control agent tool that runs a battery of pass/warn/fail checks over dataset and analysis state (record counts, data quality, parameter plausibility) and aggregates an overall QC verdict.

function:app/tools/qc_tools.py:run_qc.name: run_qc
function:app/tools/qc_tools.py:run_qc.summary: Runs the full QC checklist against pharm state, appending individual check results and issues, then returns a ToolResult with counts of passed, warned, and failed checks.

file:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py.name: regulatory_tools.py
file:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py.summary: Generates an ICH/FDA Module 2.7.2 (Summary of Clinical Pharmacology) Word document from analysis state, assembling narrative sections (background, NCA, popPK, special populations, DDI, conclusions) and standardized tables.

function:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py:g.name: generate_272
function:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py:g.summary: Tool entrypoint that assembles the full 2.7.2 document — title page, provenance, all narrative sections and summary tables — saves it to disk, and returns a ToolResult with the report path and study info.

function:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py:_.name: _section_24_special_pops
function:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py:_.summary: Builds the special-populations section by classifying selected covariates (renal, hepatic, age, weight, sex, other) into subsections and rendering each covariate effect narrative.

function:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py:_.name: _section_26_conclusions
function:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py:_.summary: Synthesizes a conclusions section, narrating the population model, IIV, selected covariates, and key exposure findings drawn from analysis state.

function:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py:_.name: _section_22_poppk
function:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py:_.summary: Renders the population-PK narrative subsection covering estimation method, structural model, parameter estimates, IIV, residual error, and VPC/GOF outcomes.

function:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py:_.name: _section_23_analyses
function:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py:_.summary: Renders the additional-analyses section, summarizing dose-proportionality and bioequivalence results when present in state.

function:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py:_.name: _table_2722_poppk
function:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py:_.summary: Builds Table 2.7.2.2 of population-PK parameter estimates with RSE, IIV omega, shrinkage, and residual sigma rows.

function:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py:_.name: _table_2723_covariates
function:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py:_.summary: Builds Table 2.7.2.3 listing selected covariate effects (coefficient, exponent, ΔOFV) and standalone covariate-effect screens.

function:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py:_.name: _table_2721_nca
function:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py:_.summary: Builds Table 2.7.2.1 of NCA exposure parameters (Cmax, Tmax, AUClast, AUCinf, t1/2, CL/F) grouped by dose.

function:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py:_.name: _section_21_background
function:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py:_.summary: Renders the background and overview section of the 2.7.2 document from study metadata.

function:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py:_.name: _section_22_nca
function:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py:_.summary: Renders the non-compartmental analysis narrative subsection describing exposure metrics and methodology.

function:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py:_.name: _kv_table
function:app/tools/regulatory_tools.py:_.summary: Shared helper that renders a headed key-value table into the document, or a placeholder line when there are no rows.

file:app/tools/report_tools.py.name: report_tools.py
file:app/tools/report_tools.py.summary: Generates a general analysis report Word document from state, rendering per-subject and dose-summary NCA tables, extra sections for BE and dose-proportionality, and a reproducibility/provenance section.

function:app/tools/report_tools.py:gener.name: generate_report
function:app/tools/report_tools.py:gener.summary: Tool entrypoint that builds the analysis report document — NCA summary/subject tables, extra analysis sections, and reproducibility — saves it, and returns a ToolResult with the report path.

function:app/tools/report_tools.py:_extr.name: _extra_sections
function:app/tools/report_tools.py:_extr.summary: Appends optional report sections for bioequivalence and dose-proportionality results when those analyses are present in state.

function:app/tools/report_tools.py:_repr.name: _reproducibility_section
function:app/tools/report_tools.py:_repr.summary: Renders a reproducibility section capturing package versions, git SHA, and dataset hash via collect_provenance for audit traceability.

function:app/tools/report_tools.py:_summ.name: _summary_table
function:app/tools/report_tools.py:_summ.summary: Renders the dose-grouped NCA summary statistics table into the report document.

function:app/tools/report_tools.py:_subj.name: _subject_table
function:app/tools/report_tools.py:_subj.summary: Renders the per-subject NCA parameter table, sorted by a stable subject key.

function:app/tools/report_tools.py:_dose.name: _dose_grouped_meaningfully
function:app/tools/report_tools.py:_dose.summary: Heuristic that decides whether the NCA summary contains more than one meaningful dose group, controlling whether a dose-grouped table is shown.

file:app/tools/review_tools.py.name: review_tools.py
file:app/tools/review_tools.py.summary: Reviewer agent tool that wraps the adversarial compute module, running independent recomputation and consistency checks over analysis results to surface findings.

function:app/tools/review_tools.py:adver.name: adversarial_review
function:app/tools/review_tools.py:adver.summary: Tool entrypoint that runs the adversarial review over current state and raw data, returning findings and an overall goal-met verdict as a ToolResult.

file:app/workflows.py.name: workflows.py
file:app/workflows.py.summary: Defines ordered workflow templates (currently the end-to-end nca_full pipeline) mapping steps to agent/tool assignments with human-review gates, plus a get_workflow lookup.

file:conftest.py.name: conftest.py
file:conftest.py.summary: Pytest bootstrap that inserts the backend root onto sys.path so the app package is importable during test collection.

file:pharmagent.db.name: pharmagent.db
file:pharmagent.db.summary: SQLite database file backing session persistence and audit storage for the PharmAgent backend.

file:tests/__init__.py.name: __init__.py
file:tests/__init__.py.summary: Empty package marker making the tests directory an importable Python package.

file:tests/reference/__init__.py.name: __init__.py
file:tests/reference/__init__.py.summary: Empty package marker making the reference-validation tests an importable Python package.

document:tests/reference/README.md.name: README.md
document:tests/reference/README.md.summary: Explains the reference-validation suite: how it drives the shipping compute path on canonical public datasets (Theophylline) to assert literature concordance, cross-method consistency, and optional exact tool concordance.

file:tests/reference/references.py.name: references.py
file:tests/reference/references.py.summary: Pins canonical public datasets (Theophylline) to published popPK literature consensus values with acceptance bands, plus an optional slot for the user's own NONMEM/Monolix/nlmixr2 run for exact concordance.

file:tests/reference/test_theophylline.p.name: test_theophylline.py
file:tests/reference/test_theophylline.p.summary: Reference-validation test that drives the shipping NCA + FOCE-I + SAEM path on the Theophylline dataset and asserts estimates fall within published literature bands and agree across methods.

file:tests/test_adversarial.py.name: test_adversarial.py
file:tests/test_adversarial.py.summary: Tests the adversarial review compute and its HTTP endpoint: that clean state meets the goal, that data inconsistencies are flagged at correct severities, recomputation triggers, and the review loop terminates.

file:tests/test_api.py.name: test_api.py
file:tests/test_api.py.summary: FastAPI endpoint tests covering public health/models routes, the session-and-NCA workflow, auth enforcement, CSV upload validation, and background-job submission/polling.

file:tests/test_audit_and_state.py.name: test_audit_and_state.py
file:tests/test_audit_and_state.py.summary: Unit tests verifying the audit hash-chain stays intact and detects tampering, and that PharmState enforces per-field ownership.

file:tests/test_be_compute.py.name: test_be_compute.py
file:tests/test_be_compute.py.summary: Unit tests for the bioequivalence compute, checking GMR and confidence intervals against hand-computed values for paired and parallel designs and the overall BE verdict logic.

file:tests/test_cdisc.py.name: test_cdisc.py
file:tests/test_cdisc.py.summary: Tests CDISC ADaM export: that ADPP is built in long BDS form with standard PARAMCDs and that the package zip contains valid define.xml, including the no-dataframe path.

file:tests/test_compartmental_compute.py.name: test_compartmental_compute.py
file:tests/test_compartmental_compute.py.summary: Unit tests for compartmental models: closed-form agreement and vectorization of 1-/2-cmt oral concentration, parameter recovery from a simulated fit, and grouped model selection.

file:tests/test_diagnostics.py.name: test_diagnostics.py
file:tests/test_diagnostics.py.summary: Unit tests for residual diagnostics and NPDE: identity fits give zero IWRES, PRED uses typical not individual parameters, and NPDE is well-calibrated, bounded, and deterministic for a fixed seed.

file:tests/test_dose_sweep.py.name: test_dose_sweep.py
file:tests/test_dose_sweep.py.summary: Unit tests for the dose-sweep compute: linear PK is dose-proportional, Michaelis-Menten is super-proportional, output envelope/metadata shapes are consistent, and Cavg equals AUC over tau.

file:tests/test_dp_compute.py.name: test_dp_compute.py
file:tests/test_dp_compute.py.summary: Unit tests for dose-proportionality power-model estimation: slope recovery for proportional/sub-/super-proportional data, closed-form critical region and CI, and aggregation across parameters.

file:tests/test_forecast.py.name: test_forecast.py
file:tests/test_forecast.py.summary: Unit tests for MAP/TDM forecasting: Bayesian update pulls individual estimates toward truth, no-levels falls back to population typical, dose optimization hits a target, and steady-state metrics are ordered.

file:tests/test_identity_audit.py.name: test_identity_audit.py
file:tests/test_identity_audit.py.summary: Tests identity-aware tamper-evident auditing: actor/reason recording with chain verification, tampering detection, backward compatibility, provenance stamping, and signed human-review entries.

file:tests/test_nca_compute.py.name: test_nca_compute.py
file:tests/test_nca_compute.py.summary: Unit tests for non-compartmental analysis: exact lambda_z and t1/2, AUCinf against analytic truth, Cmax/Tmax, CL/V, small percent-extrapolation, and dose grouping.

file:tests/test_nlme.py.name: test_nlme.py
file:tests/test_nlme.py.summary: Comprehensive unit tests for the NLME estimation engine: simulates synthetic populations with known parameters and verifies FOCE-I and SAEM recover theta/omega/sigma, plus covariate models, SCM selection, parameter uncertainty (RSE), and M3 BLQ censored-likelihood handling.

file:tests/test_nmexport.py.name: test_nmexport.py
file:tests/test_nmexport.py.summary: Unit tests for NONMEM control-stream and mrgsolve C++ code generation, asserting correct ADVAN/TRANS selection, power-model covariate THETA blocks, ALAG lag-time handling on depot, and None return for unsupported models.

file:tests/test_orchestrator.py.name: test_orchestrator.py
file:tests/test_orchestrator.py.summary: Integration tests for the agent Orchestrator and Supervisor: verifies LLM-based routing, workflow pause at the human-review gate, resume-to-report completion, privacy (metadata-only), SQLite persistence across restart, and session ownership enforcement.

file:tests/test_pk_models.py.name: test_pk_models.py
file:tests/test_pk_models.py.summary: Unit tests for the 18-model PK/PKPD library: validates analytic closed-form agreement (IV/oral 1-2cmt), allometric scaling, Michaelis-Menten saturability, transit-compartment peak delay, Emax/IDR PKPD behavior, parameter fitting, and AIC-based model comparison.

file:tests/test_poppk_compute.py.name: test_poppk_compute.py
file:tests/test_poppk_compute.py.summary: Unit tests for two-stage popPK summary statistics: closed-form verification of geometric mean, geometric CV%, typical-value/IIV summarization, and log-linear covariate-effect estimation with edge cases for nonpositive and insufficient data.

file:tests/test_report_272.py.name: test_report_272.py
file:tests/test_report_272.py.summary: Tests for the FDA Section 2.7.2 regulatory report generator: validates placeholder handling for empty state, NCA/NLME/SCM table population, study-info propagation, reproducibility section, and HTTP endpoints for report generation and download.

file:tests/test_skills.py.name: test_skills.py
file:tests/test_skills.py.summary: Tests for the skill capture/replay subsystem: verifies step distillation (dropping I/O and profiling, collapsing duplicates), SkillStore save/get/list/delete with versioning, markdown rendering, and end-to-end replay reproducing an NCA workflow on a fresh session, including HTTP routes.

file:tests/test_ss_compute.py.name: test_ss_compute.py
file:tests/test_ss_compute.py.summary: Unit tests for steady-state PK computation: confirms SS concentration formulas match single-dose superposition and reduce correctly at large tau, SS fitting recovers parameters, multiple-dose detection, and steady-state NCA grouping by dose.

file:tests/test_vpc.py.name: test_vpc.py
file:tests/test_vpc.py.summary: Unit tests for VPC and goodness-of-fit diagnostics: validates CV-to-SD conversion, observed-vs-predicted GOF metrics, simulation-based VPC band ordering and lognormal spread, determinism under fixed seed, and prediction-corrected VPC (pcVPC) structure and coverage.

function:tests/test_nlme.py:_make_popula.name: _make_population
function:tests/test_nlme.py:_make_popula.summary: Simulates a synthetic NLME population with known CL/V/KA typical values and lognormal IIV plus residual error, returning subject records used as ground truth for parameter-recovery assertions.

function:tests/test_nlme.py:_make_cov_po.name: _make_cov_population
function:tests/test_nlme.py:_make_cov_po.summary: Builds a synthetic population whose clearance depends on a weight covariate with a known exponent, used to test covariate-model recovery and stepwise covariate modeling selection.

function:tests/test_nlme.py:_censored_po.name: _censored_population
function:tests/test_nlme.py:_censored_po.summary: Generates a population with concentrations censored below an LLOQ and a parallel BLQ flag array, used to test the M3 censored-likelihood estimation path.

function:tests/test_nlme.py:test_sparse_.name: test_sparse_subject_is_handled
function:tests/test_nlme.py:test_sparse_.summary: Verifies FOCE-I fitting tolerates a subject with very few observations without crashing and still returns finite estimates for the full cohort.

function:tests/test_nlme.py:test_scm_sel.name: test_scm_selects_true_covariate_and_rejects_noise
function:tests/test_nlme.py:test_scm_sel.summary: Runs stepwise covariate modeling on a synthetic population and asserts the true weight-on-CL covariate is selected while a noise covariate is rejected.

function:tests/test_nmexport.py:_fit.name: _fit
function:tests/test_nmexport.py:_fit.summary: Helper that constructs a minimal fitted-model dict (model key, theta, IIV, covariate spec) used as input to the NONMEM and mrgsolve code builders under test.

function:tests/test_orchestrator.py:test.name: test_persistence_survives_restart
function:tests/test_orchestrator.py:test.summary: Creates a session and workflow under one Orchestrator backed by an on-disk SessionStore, then reconstructs a fresh Orchestrator and asserts the session and intact audit chain are recovered.

function:tests/test_pk_models.py:test_pk.name: test_pkpd_dual_endpoint_fit_recovers
function:tests/test_pk_models.py:test_pk.summary: Simulates a coupled PK/PD dual-endpoint dataset and verifies the joint fitter recovers the known PK and PD parameters within tolerance.

function:tests/test_pk_models.py:test_si.name: test_simulate_timecourse_single_and_multidose
function:tests/test_pk_models.py:test_si.summary: Checks the timecourse simulator produces correct single-dose and multiple-dose accumulation profiles across the model library.

function:tests/test_poppk_compute.py:tes.name: test_two_stage_summary_typical_value_and_iiv
function:tests/test_poppk_compute.py:tes.summary: Verifies the two-stage summary computes the correct geometric-mean typical value and inter-individual variability from per-subject parameter estimates.

function:tests/test_poppk_compute.py:tes.name: test_covariate_effect_perfect_log_linear
function:tests/test_poppk_compute.py:tes.summary: Constructs a perfect log-linear covariate relationship and asserts the covariate-effect estimator recovers the exact slope.

function:tests/test_report_272.py:_nlme_.name: _nlme_state
function:tests/test_report_272.py:_nlme_.summary: Builds a synthetic PharmState populated with NLME results, used to test that the Section 2.7.2 report renders model tables and conclusions.

function:tests/test_report_272.py:test_f.name: test_full_state_no_crash
function:tests/test_report_272.py:test_f.summary: Smoke test asserting the 2.7.2 report generator runs end-to-end without error on a fully populated state combining NCA, NLME, and SCM results.

function:tests/test_report_272.py:test_h.name: test_http_272_download
function:tests/test_report_272.py:test_h.summary: Exercises the HTTP endpoint that downloads a generated 2.7.2 report file via the FastAPI TestClient.

function:tests/test_skills.py:test_http_.name: test_http_capture_list_get_run_delete
function:tests/test_skills.py:test_http_.summary: End-to-end HTTP test covering the full skill lifecycle: capture from a session, list, get, run (replay), and delete via the FastAPI TestClient.

function:tests/test_skills.py:test_captu.name: test_capture_then_inspect_steps
function:tests/test_skills.py:test_captu.summary: Captures a skill from an executed NCA session and asserts the distilled steps reflect the tool calls performed.

function:tests/test_ss_compute.py:test_e.name: test_extract_multiple_dose
function:tests/test_ss_compute.py:test_e.summary: Verifies steady-state dosing-interval extraction correctly identifies multiple-dose regimens and partitions the profile into per-tau intervals.

function:tests/test_vpc.py:_make_subject.name: _make_subject
function:tests/test_vpc.py:_make_subject.summary: Helper that builds a single subject record with given parameters and weight covariate for use across the VPC and GOF tests.

function:tests/test_vpc.py:_correct_mode.name: _correct_model_population
function:tests/test_vpc.py:_correct_mode.summary: Simulates a population from the correct model so that pcVPC band coverage of the observed data can be validated.

function:tests/test_vpc.py:test_obs_vs_p.name: test_obs_vs_pred_identity_is_perfect_fit
function:tests/test_vpc.py:test_obs_vs_p.summary: Asserts that when individual predictions equal observations, the observed-vs-predicted GOF metrics report a perfect fit.

function:tests/test_vpc.py:test_vpc_band.name: test_vpc_band_lognormal_spread_matches_cv
function:tests/test_vpc.py:test_vpc_band.summary: Verifies the simulated VPC band spread matches the lognormal IIV implied by the specified coefficient of variation.
